title: dagr.run@1
description: The C22 run artifact: the outcome of one execution, joinable to the structure, derived from the event stream. A single canonical JSON document (T4 §2) with a header (run identity, pipeline identity, both fingerprint hashes, invocation parameters + data interval, allowlisted captured environment, resume lineage, overall outcome), one record PER ATTEMPT, and a summary. Accommodates the assembly-failed / bootstrap-failed variants (no fingerprint, non-empty error list, zero attempts) and the single-node replay variant (`not-requested` node marking). Published by T39 (ticket 050). Schema evolution is additive-only within a version (T0.10): no object sets additionalProperties:false, so unknown future fields validate.

attempts.description: One record PER ATTEMPT (retries are the interesting signal, C22). Empty for the pre-execution failure variants.

summary.description: Run summary (C22). Null for the pre-execution failure variants (zero attempts).

variant.description: Optional artifact variant marker. `single-node-replay` marks a C26 single-node replay artifact whose out-of-request nodes carry the `not-requested` marking.

requested_node.description: Single-node-replay: the node the replay was requested for.

node_markings.description: Single-node-replay: per-node markings. `not-requested` is an ARTIFACT MARKING for nodes outside the request (C22/C26), distinct from any terminal state.

allOf.description: A pre-execution failure (assembly-failed or bootstrap-failed) carries a non-empty error list, no fingerprint, and zero attempts (C22).

not.description: A fingerprint is present ONLY when assembly succeeded, so a pre-execution failure must NOT carry either fingerprint hash.

attempts.description: Zero attempts for a pre-execution failure.

fingerprint_structural.description: Structural fingerprint (C21), present ONLY when assembly succeeded. Cross-references a graph artifact from the same build.

captured_environment.description: Allowlisted captured environment values only, default allowlist empty (C22). Each value is a captured scalar (string/number/boolean); the schema does NOT sanction unbounded (nested-structure) environment capture.

resume_lineage.description: Resume lineage: immediate parent and lineage-root run identities, when resumed; null otherwise (C22/C27).

overall_outcome.description: The overall run outcome. `assembly-failed` and `bootstrap-failed` are DISTINCT pre-execution-failure values (one the graph's fault, the other the machine's).

errors.description: The complete error list for a pre-execution failure variant (non-empty when present).

attempt.description: One attempt record (C22): node identity, attempt number, terminal status from the normative taxonomy, named phase durations, worker, message, structured error detail, reported metrics, declared-vs-measured cost, and the optional durable-output reference (T0.8).

status.description: Terminal status from the normative taxonomy (arch.md Vocabulary). `not-requested` is NOT here — it is an artifact marking, never a terminal state.

phase_durations_ns.description: Named phase durations in nanoseconds, computed from monotonic offsets so phases sum to the attempt total exactly (C22).

error.description: Structured error detail (T3 error taxonomy), when the attempt did not succeed.

metrics.description: Reported metrics — an open numeric map (C23). Keys are metric names carrying units by convention; values are numeric.

durable_reference.description: The durable-output reference (T0.8), present only on a durable node's succeeded attempt or copied forward on resume. OPAQUE to the schema — whatever the task's Reference type serializes to.

satisfied_from_run.description: The ORIGINATING run identity a satisfied-from-prior attempt carries (C22/C27). Required when status is satisfied-from-prior (enforced below).

then.description: A satisfied-from-prior record MUST carry its originating run identity (C22/C27).

summary.description: Run summary (C22): total elapsed, critical path, peak slot residency, retained values, abandoned-work-pinned time and capacity.
